content.problem_latex:
一块均匀生长的草地按照$$1:2:3$$的面积比分成三块．一群牛先用$$12$$天时间吃完了第一块草地的草，接着又用$$48$$天吃完了第二块草地的草．此时，这群牛需要（~ ）天能够吃完第三块草地的草．
(A) 216
(B) 288
(C) 324
(D) 396
content.problem_plain: 一块均匀生长的草地按照 1:2:3 的面积比分成三块．一群牛先用 12 天时间吃完了第一块草地的草，接着又用 48 天吃完了第二块草地的草．此时，这群牛需要（~ ）天能够吃完第三块草地的草． (A) 216 (B) 288 (C) 324 (D) 396
content.solution_latex: 设$$1$$份草地原有草量为$$x$$，每天生长量为$$y$$，第三块草地需要$$n$$天吃完．  根据牛每天吃的草量相等可得：  $$\frac{x+12y}{12}=\frac{2x+2\left( 12y+48y \right)}{48}$$  解得$$x=36y$$，这群牛的每天吃草量为$$4y$$．  故第三块草地的等量关系为$$\frac{3x+3\left( 12y+48y+ny \right)}{4y}=n$$，解得$$n=288$$．
content.solution_plain: 设 1 份草地原有草量为 x ，每天生长量为 y ，第三块草地需要 n 天吃完． 根据牛每天吃的草量相等可得： x+12y 12 = 2x+2 ( 12y+48y ) 48 解得 x=36y ，这群牛的每天吃草量为 4y ． 故第三块草地的等量关系为 3x+3 ( 12y+48y+ny ) 4y =n ，解得 n=288 ．

step_outline: 设$$1$$份草地原有草量为$$x$$，每天生长量为$$y$$，第三块草地需要$$n$$天吃完．  根据牛每天吃的草量相等可得：  $$\frac{x+12y}{12}=\frac{2x+2\left( 12y+48y \right)}{48}$$  解得$$x=36y$$，这群牛的每天吃草量为$$4y$$．  故第三块草地的等量关系为$$\frac{3x+3\left( 12y+48y+ny \right)}{4y}=n$$，解得$$n=288$$．

content.problem_latex:
2002年$$AMC10$$竞赛$$A$$第$$19$$题  Spot\textquotesingle s doghouse has a regular hexagonal base that measures one yard on each side. He is tethered to a vertex with a two-yard rope. What is the area, in square yards, of the region outside of the doghouse that Spot can reach?  Spot 的狗窝有一个规则的六边形底座，每边长一码。 他被一根两码长的绳子拴在一个顶点上。 Spot 可以到达的狗窝外区域的面积是多少平方码？
(A) \frac {2\pi }3
(B) 2\pi
(C) \frac {5\pi }2
(D) \frac {8\pi} 3
(E) 3\pi
content.problem_plain: 2002年 AMC10 竞赛 A 第 19 题 Spot s doghouse has a regular hexagonal base that measures one yard on each side. He is tethered to a vertex with a two-yard rope. What is the area, in square yards, of the region outside of the doghouse that Spot can reach? Spot 的狗窝有一个规则的六边形底座，每边长一码。 他被一根两码长的绳子拴在一个顶点上。 Spot 可以到达的狗窝外区域的面积是多少平方码？ (A) 2 3 (B) 2 (C) 5 2 (D) 8 3 (E) 3
content.solution_latex: Part of what Spot can reach is $$\frac {240}{360}=\frac 23$$ of a circle with radius $$2$$, which gives him $$\frac {8\pi }3$$. He can also reach two $$\frac {60}{360}$$ parts of a unit circle, which combines to give $$\frac {\pi }3$$. The total area is then $$3\pi $$, which gives $$\rm E$$.  Note   We can clearly see that the area must be more than $$\frac {8\pi }3$$, and the only such answer is $$\rm E$$.
content.solution_plain: Part of what Spot can reach is 240 360 = 23 of a circle with radius 2 , which gives him 8 3 . He can also reach two 60 360 parts of a unit circle, which combines to give 3 . The total area is then 3 , which gives E . Note We can clearly see that the area must be more than 8 3 , and the only such answer is E .

step_outline: Part of what Spot can reach is $$\frac {240}{360}=\frac 23$$ of a circle with radius $$2$$, which gives him $$\frac {8\pi }3$$.
He can also reach two $$\frac {60}{360}$$ parts of a unit circle, which combines to give $$\frac {\pi }3$$.
The total area is then $$3\pi $$, which gives $$\rm E$$.
Note   We can clearly see that the area must be more than $$\frac {8\pi }3$$, and the only such answer is $$\rm E$$.

The total area is then 3 , which gives E . Note We can clearly see that the area must be more than 8 3 , and the only such answer is E .

content.problem_latex:
若集合：$${{S}_{1}}=\left { \left( x,  y \right)\textbar\lg \left( 1+{{x}^{2}}+{{y}^{2}} \right)\leqslant 1+\lg \left( x+y \right) \right }$$，$${{S}_{2}}=\left { \left( x,  y \right)\textbar\lg \left( 2+{{x}^{2}}+{{y}^{2}} \right)\leqslant 2+\lg \left( x+y \right) \right }$$，则$${{S}_{2}}$$的面积与$${{S}_{1}}$$的面积比为
(A) 99:1
(B) 100:1
(C) 101:1
(D) 102:1
content.solution_latex: $${{S}_{1}}$$表示圆内部（包括圆周）：$${{\left( x-5 \right)}^{2}}+{{\left( y-5 \right)}^{2}}\leqslant 49$$，面积为$$49 \pi $$．$${{S}_{2}}$$表示圆内部（包括圆周）：$${{\left( x-50 \right)}^{2}}+{{\left( y-50 \right)}^{2}}\leqslant 4998$$，面积为$$4998 \pi $$．因此，面积比为$$102:1$$．

step_outline: $${{S}_{1}}$$表示圆内部（包括圆周）：$${{\left( x-5 \right)}^{2}}+{{\left( y-5 \right)}^{2}}\leqslant 49$$，面积为$$49 \pi $$．$${{S}_{2}}$$表示圆内部（包括圆周）：$${{\left( x-50 \right)}^{2}}+{{\left( y-50 \right)}^{2}}\leqslant 4998$$，面积为$$4998 \pi $$．因此，面积比为$$102:1$$．

content.problem_latex:
2009年$$AMC10$$竞赛$$A$$第$$19$$题  Circle $$A$$ has radius $$100$$. Circle $$B$$ has an integer radius $$r\textless{} 100$$ and remains internally tangent to circle $$A$$ as it rolls once around the circumference of circle $$A$$. The two circles have the same points of tangency at the beginning and end of circle $$B\textquotesingle\rm s$$ trip. How many possible values can $$r$$ have?  圆 $$A$$ 的半径为 $$100$$。 圆 $$B$$ 有一个整数半径 $$r\textless{} 100$$ 并且在它绕圆 $$A$$ 的圆周滚动一次时保持与圆 $$A$$ 内切。 这两个圆在圆 $$B$$ 轨迹的起点和终点具有相同的切点。 $$r$$ 可以有多少个可能的值？
(A) 4
(B) 8
(C) 9
(D) 50
(E) 90
content.problem_plain: 2009年 AMC10 竞赛 A 第 19 题 Circle A has radius 100 . Circle B has an integer radius r 100 and remains internally tangent to circle A as it rolls once around the circumference of circle A . The two circles have the same points of tangency at the beginning and end of circle B s trip. How many possible values can r have? 圆 A 的半径为 100 。 圆 B 有一个整数半径 r 100 并且在它绕圆 A 的圆周滚动一次时保持与圆 A 内切。 这两个圆在圆 B 轨迹的起点和终点具有相同的切点。 r 可以有多少个可能的值？ (A) 4 (B) 8 (C) 9 (D) 50 (E) 90
content.solution_latex: The circumference of circle $$A$$ is $$200\pi$$ , and the circumference of circle $$B$$ with radius r is $$2r\pi$$. Since circle $$B$$ makes a complete revolution and ends up on the same point, the circumference of $$A$$ must be a multiple of the circumference of $$B$$, therefore the quotient must be an integer. Thus, $$\dfrac{200 \pi}{2 \pi \cdot r}= \dfrac{100}{r}$$. Therefore $$r$$ must then be a factor of $$100$$, excluding $$100$$ (because then circle $$B$$ would be the same size as circle $$A$$). $$100 =2^{2}\cdot5^{2}$$. Therefore $$100$$ has $$(2+1)\cdot (2+1)$$ factors*. But you need to subtract $$1$$ from $$9$$, in order to exclude $$100$$. Therefore the answer is $$8$$.
content.solution_plain: The circumference of circle A is 200 , and the circumference of circle B with radius r is 2r . Since circle B makes a complete revolution and ends up on the same point, the circumference of A must be a multiple of the circumference of B , therefore the quotient must be an integer. Thus, 200 2 r = 100 r . Therefore r must then be a factor of 100 , excluding 100 (because then circle B would be the same size as circle A ). 100 =2^ 2 5^ 2 . Therefore 100 has (2+1) (2+1) factors*. But you need to subtract 1 from 9 , in order to exclude 100 . Therefore the answer is 8 .

step_outline: The circumference of circle $$A$$ is $$200\pi$$ , and the circumference of circle $$B$$ with radius r is $$2r\pi$$.
Since circle $$B$$ makes a complete revolution and ends up on the same point, the circumference of $$A$$ must be a multiple of the circumference of $$B$$, therefore the quotient must be an integer.
Therefore $$r$$ must then be a factor of $$100$$, excluding $$100$$ (because then circle $$B$$ would be the same size as circle $$A$$).
But you need to subtract $$1$$ from $$9$$, in order to exclude $$100$$.

Circle B has an integer radius r 100 and remains internally tangent to circle A as it rolls once around the circumference of circle A . The two circles have the same points of tangency at the beginning and end of circle B s trip. How many possible values can r have? 圆 A 的半径为 100 。 圆 B 有一个整数半径 r 100 并且在它绕圆 A 的圆周滚动一次时保持与圆 A 内切。 这两个圆在圆 B 轨迹的起点和终点具有相同的切点。 r 可以有多少个可能的值？ (A) 4 (B) 8 (C) 9 (D) 50 (E) 90
search.solution_text: The circumference of circle A is 200 , and the circumference of circle B with radius r is 2r . Since circle B makes a complete revolution and ends up on the same point, the circumference of A must be a multiple of the circumference of B , therefore the quotient must be an integer. Thus, 200 2 r = 100 r . Therefore r must then be a factor of 100 , excluding 100 (because then circle B would be the same size as circle A ). 100 =2^ 2 5^ 2 . Therefore 100 has (2+1) (2+1) factors*. But you need to subtract 1 from 9 , in order to exclude 100 . Therefore the answer is 8 .
